concept: SORTED BY A MACHINE: a top-down look at the spring-2025 purge in which an LLM was used to answer one blunt question, does this grant relate at all to DEI, over more than a thousand research proposals, flagging and terminating an Alaska Native Language Archive grant among about fourteen hundred, until a court ruled the cuts unlawful and reinstatement began in June 2026, honest that the model did not decide, people did, and that offered back is not the same as restored.
palette: institutional cold-grey fluorescent, manila tan dossier, crimson termination mark, honey-amber archive warmth, evergreen court reversal, slate modular grid

fingerprint.palette: institutional cold-grey fluorescent, manila tan dossier, crimson termination mark, honey-amber archive warmth, evergreen court reversal, slate modular grid
fingerprint.metaphor: a crude machine sorting a people's memory into keep and delete

light.sun: hard, high, cold overhead fluorescent raking straight down on the grid, flat shadowless bureaucratic light; the archive shots break to a warm honey-amber practical glowing from the recordings themselves
light.mood: the cold institutional light IS the point, a spreadsheet with no warmth; warmth exists only inside the human archive

hook.frame1: a top-down grid of a thousand pale grant cards under a cold scanning beam, one card already lit crimson
hook.headline: SORTED BY A MACHINE
hook.loopback: the film ends on one of those cards, now a warm archive being read by a human hand, line by line

hero.name: the grant grid
hero.detail_note: a top-down modular grid of pale manila grant cards, a bright scanner bar sweeping, sorted piles forming, the machine head at the edge

shots.note: establish the machine triage of a thousand grants

hero.name: the classifier head
hero.detail_note: a card in a slot, a single prompt line, a DEI gate that flips YES or NO, a stamp arm that cocks

shots.note: the LLM as a blunt yes/no rubber stamp

hero.name: the language archive card
hero.detail_note: one flagged card blooms into a warm field of amber voice-waveform bars (recorded speech), fragile flickers, a crimson stamp descending

shots.note: the archive as a people's living memory; the stamp falls

hero.name: the terminated field
hero.detail_note: crimson terminations spreading radially across the grid, a tally climbing to 1,400, hard numerals 100M and 22 DAYS, emptied slots

hero.name: the reversal
hero.detail_note: left: the stamp arm revealed as driven by a human hand on a lever; right: a court-green wave lifting the crimson and relighting the archive card

shots.note: a person aimed it; a court reversed it

hero.name: the read archive
hero.detail_note: the warm waveform archive with dim gaps of lost recordings, a human hand tracing the restored lines line by line, the ALASKA.AI outro

shots.note: offered back is not restored; a human reads every line; outro

beats.vo: Not long ago, more than a thousand research grants were sorted by a machine.
beats.shows: top-down over a vast modular grid of pale grant cards, a bright scanner bar sweeping across
beats.means: a bureaucracy handed to a machine

choreo.primary: the scanner bar sweeps across the grid

beats.vo: The question it was asked was simple. Does this relate, at all, to D.E.I.?
beats.shows: the scanner readout: one blunt prompt line typing on over the grid
beats.means: one blunt yes or no question

beats.shows: a single grant card lifts out of the grid and slides toward the machine slot

choreo.primary: a card rises and slides to the slot

beats.vo: Each proposal went to a chatbot, which answered in a single word.
beats.shows: data-panel: the classifier head, the card in its slot, a DEI gate lit
beats.means: a language model used as a rubber stamp

choreo.primary: the card seats in the slot

beats.shows: a hard binary: YES lights one side, NO the other, a stamp arm cocking
beats.means: a life's work reduced to one bit

beats.vo: One of them was a plan to save the Alaska Native Language Archive.
beats.shows: focus-pull to macro: one card blooms into a field of glowing amber voice-waveform bars
beats.means: this one held a people's memory

choreo.primary: the card blooms into warm waveforms

beats.vo: Decades of elders' voices, the last fluent recordings of whole languages.
beats.shows: the amber waveform field deepens, receding rows of recordings

choreo.primary: rows of waveforms recede into depth

beats.shows: a heavy crimson stamp descends over the glowing archive

beats.vo: The chatbot answered yes, it relates.
beats.shows: the stamp lands: TERMINATED in crimson across the archive card, waveforms greying
beats.means: flagged out of existence by a word

beats.vo: So the grant was terminated, along with about fourteen hundred others.
beats.shows: pull-out to the grid: crimson terminations spread across the field, a tally climbing to 1,400
beats.means: a mass cut, not one mistake

beats.vo: A hundred million dollars, gone in twenty-two days.
beats.shows: hard numerals slam over the reddened grid: 100 MILLION DOLLARS, 22 DAYS
beats.means: the scale and speed of it

beats.vo: But the chatbot did not decide. A person aimed it, and a person signed.
beats.shows: two-up: the machine stamp on the left, a human hand closing on the lever on the right
beats.means: accountability is human, not the model

choreo.primary: a human hand closes on the lever
choreo.reaction: the stamp is revealed as hand-driven

beats.vo: Then a court ruled the cuts unlawful, and this summer the funding was offered back.
beats.shows: a court-green wave sweeps across, lifting the crimson stamps, the archive card relighting

beats.vo: Offered back is not restored. The lost year does not return.
beats.shows: the archive card warm again but with visible dim gaps where bars are still missing

choreo.primary: slow push in on the card
choreo.reaction: missing bars flicker out as the push reaches them

beats.vo: An archive of a language is memory. It deserves a human reading every line.
beats.shows: a human hand moves along the restored waveforms, reading line by line, the brand outro begins
beats.means: a human must stay in the loop

choreo.primary: the hand traces along the lines
choreo.reaction: bars light warm as they are read

divergence_note: This breaks hard from the last two dispatches and from the recent naturalistic 3D worlds. 07-12 (beluga hoax) was an eye-level, single-organic-hero, horizontal-traverse orbit through water; 07-11 (smoke lid) was an orbital-aerial, landscape-as-subject, vertical-descent into a valley. This is a TOP-DOWN-MAP onto a GRID-MODULAR field of grant records that SCROLL-DATA under a machine scanner, its register is DATA-BRUTALIST (hard Swiss grid, numerals-forward) not naturalistic, its hero is DATA (records) not a creature or a landscape, its camera strategy is TRACK-FOLLOW not orbit-reveal, and its light is NOON-HARD cold fluorescent rather than storm or night, on a fresh institutional grey/manila/crimson/amber/court-green palette that shares no color world with smoke ochre or aquarium magenta. The cold machine grid is deliberately set against a warm amber human archive, and the caveat that the model did not decide is DRAWN as a human hand on the lever. The spatial signature (top-down-map, grid-modular, scroll-data) has not appeared in the last four dispatches.
